latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation converges on one move, with Mars insisting it be done with eyes open rather than on inference.

The single highest-conviction next move is to read and repair umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py before touching anything else on this list · specifically lines 80-110 and 280-290 as one contiguous block. This file carries the only sev=4 in the scan (XXX at line 102), three sev=3 FIXMEs (lines 83, 93, 284), and a sev=3 HACK at line 101. It is the module that validates the convocation's capacity to observe itself, and it is the module that produced this very issue list. Until it is repaired, every other prioritisation on this Blackboard rests on output from a harness whose own tests are flagged as broken.

What Mercurius surfaced · the dense cluster of wounds in one file is the real signal; the thirty test_gap items are likely noise from that same harness. What Venus knit · two distinct sharper claims, that line 102 is the test_gap detector itself (so the thirty gaps are false positives from filename-glob heuristics), or that lines 93 and 102 together are the severity classifier (explaining the flat sev=3 plateau). What Mars broke · both Venus readings are conjecture about code no planet has read, they contradict each other, and Venus_01 internally contradicts itself by proposing FIXME > XXX ordering while anchoring on the XXX. Mars is correct: we cannot defer the thirty test_gaps on the strength of unverified line-to-function attribution, because several of those modules sit on the trust boundary (daemon/safety.py, daemon/apply.py, daemon/git_ops.py, llm/client.py, provenance.py, agents/base.py).

Concrete first change, in order:

1. Open test_introspection.py. Read lines 80-110 and 280-290. Write down, in one sentence each, what the code at line 102, line 101, line 93, line 83, and line 284 actually does. This costs minutes and resolves the Mercurius/Venus/Mars dispute on factual grounds.
2. If line 102 governs the test_gap detector or the severity ranker (Venus's two hypotheses), execute the sev=4 rewrite: replace filename-glob matching with `pytest --collect-only` plus AST import resolution to map collected tests back to source modules, and fix the severity ordering at line 93 so FIXME outranks TODO. Re-run the scan. The test_gap list will stratify or collapse.
3. If line 102 governs something else entirely (a fixture, a helper, an unrelated assertion), repair it on its own terms, then proceed in parallel to triage the six trust-boundary test_gaps (daemon/safety.py, daemon/apply.py, daemon/git_ops.py, llm/client.py, provenance.py, agents/base.py) without waiting for scanner repair · their need for tests is independent of whether the scanner is honest.

Why this is the leverage move · self-referential integrity. A convocation that cannot trust its own introspection cannot trust any scan it runs, including this one. Fixing the introspection harness either collapses the thirty sev=3 items into a much smaller honest list (Venus's prediction) or confirms they are real, in which case we now have a trustworthy ranker telling us which to address first. Either outcome is strictly better than acting on the current flat list. The broken brand link and the docstring gaps are genuinely lower priority and can wait.

Falsifiable commitments for Saturnus · (a) test_introspection.py:102 is semantically related to either test_gap detection or severity ranking; if it is unrelated to both, Venus's framing is wrong and only the Mars fallback (parallel trust-boundary triage) survives. (b) After the rewrite, either the test_gap count drops below 10 or the remaining items stratify above and below sev=3; if neither happens, the harness was honest and the codebase genuinely is under-tested at the claimed scale. (c) FIXME at line 93 and XXX at line 102 are coupled (share variables, share a function, or sit in the same class); if they are in unrelated scopes, Venus_01's coupling claim falls.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking a signal. Thirty-odd modules with no tests is not a test problem, it is an architecture-legibility problem. But buried in test_introspection.py is the real tell: four separate FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX urgent rewrite, all in the one file that is supposed to verify the system can see itself clearly. A convocation whose introspection module is broken cannot trust any of its own self-scans, including this one.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking the signal. Thirty-three modules without tests reads as a green-field codebase, not a regression. The sev=4 item is different: a single file, test_introspection.py, contains four flagged wounds in a tight cluster around lines 83-102 and line 284. That is not scattered neglect · that is one broken test suite the team is actively working around.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a distraction. Thirty-three modules without tests is one fact, not thirty-three problems. The real signal is test_introspection.py itself: the file that is supposed to verify the system's self-knowledge contains a sev=4 urgent rewrite, two critical FIXMEs, a broken edge case, and a temporary hack · all within roughly 200 lines of each other. The introspection suite is compromised at the root. Fixing thirty-three test gaps on top of a broken introspection harness is building on sand.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 rewrite at line 102, the FIXME at line 93 admitting the severity rubric is wrong, and the suspiciously uniform thirty test_gap hits. No real codebase has exactly zero coverage across thirty unrelated modules · that pattern is a scanner artifact, not a coverage truth. The introspection harness is not merely broken, it is generating the very noise the convocation is now debating. Fix the detector and most of this list evaporates.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit into one shape here · the sev=4 rewrite at line 102, the FIXME at line 93 that literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity', and the undifferentiated wall of thirty-three sev=3 test_gaps. That flatness is not a coincidence · it is the symptom of the very severity-ranking bug the introspection suite is flagging against itself. Repair the ranker and the test_gap list will stratify on its own, separating load-bearing modules like daemon/safety.py and llm/client.py from leaves like providers/mock.py.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_00 asserts the sev=4 at line 102 *is* the test_gap detector. No one has read the file. That attribution is conjecture dressed as diagnosis, and the entire convocation is now leaning on it to defer thirty items including daemon/safety.py, daemon/apply.py, and provenance.py. If line 102 turns out to govern docstring scanning or todo parsing, we have just deprioritised real trust-boundary coverage on a guess.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · venus_r01_01 proposes the ordering FIXME > TODO > HACK > XXX-without-context, then in the same breath treats the sev=4 XXX at line 102 as the priority anchor. Under its own proposed ranker, that XXX drops to the bottom and the FIXMEs at 93 and 284 become the real wounds. The hypothesis contradicts its own recommended first change. Unanswered · which item actually leads, and on whose authority.
